name: Mexican Vegetarian Stuffed Peppers
instructions:
1. Note: Ensure the Brown Rice is cooked before starting this recipe. Preheat the oven to 350 degrees F (175 degrees C).

2. Bring a large pot of Water with some Salt to a boil. Add the green Bell Peppers and cook until slightly softened but still holding their shape, 3 to 4 minutes. The peppers should be tender enough to stuff but not mushy. Drain the Bell Peppers well and arrange them cut-side up in a baking dish.

3. Heat the Olive Oil in a skillet over medium heat. Add the diced Onion and cook, stirring occasionally, until softened and translucent, 5 to 10 minutes. The onion should be fragrant and lightly golden.

4. Transfer the cooked Onion into a large bowl. Add the cooked Brown Rice, Canned Black Beans (drained and rinsed), and canned chili-style diced Tomatoes with their juices. Stir in the Chili Powder, Garlic Salt, Cumin, and remaining Salt until everything is well combined and aromatic. Fold in most of the Mexican Cheese Blend, reserving some for topping.

5. Spoon the rice mixture evenly into each Bell Pepper, packing it gently but firmly. Sprinkle the remaining Mexican Cheese Blend generously over the top of each stuffed pepper.

6. Bake in the preheated oven until the cheese is melted and bubbling, and the peppers are tender, about 30 minutes. The cheese should be golden and the filling heated through. Let rest for a few minutes before serving.
simpleInstructions:
1. Note: Cook Brown Rice before starting. Preheat oven to 350 degrees F (175 degrees C). Boil Bell Peppers in salted Water until slightly softened.

2. Cook diced Onion in Olive Oil until soft. Mix with cooked Brown Rice, Canned Black Beans, diced Tomatoes, spices, and most of the Mexican Cheese Blend.

3. Stuff Bell Peppers with the mixture, top with remaining cheese, and bake until melted and bubbly.
